title: celtis occidentalis var. audibertiana
intro: Celtis occidentalis var. audibertiana, also known as the Audibert's hackberry, is a variety of the hackberry tree native to North America, characterized by its small, deciduous leaves and edible fruits. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used in traditional herbal medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Celtis occidentalis var. audibertiana include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to make herbal preparations for topical and internal use.
